summary: Matched the Peach Toad Spore item unit by replacing placeholder/nonmatching stubs in itpeachtoadspore.c with C implementations, adding the needed special-attributes struct and more precise header prototypes, then flipping the object from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The decomp.dev bot reported 10 newly matched functions in main/melee/it/items/itpeachtoadspore, totaling +868 matched bytes and moving the unit functions from 0% to 100%.

key_files.role: Main matched implementation for Peach Toad Spore item lifecycle, spawn, velocity, timer, effects, shield bounce, and cleanup callbacks.
Updated prototypes from generic/unknown signatures to typed Item_GObj, ItemKind, f32, bool, and void signatures for matched functions.
Added itPeachToadSporeAttributes with min speed, max speed offset, speed decay rate, and angle fields used by the item implementation.
Changed melee/it/items/itpeachtoadspore.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...).

what_changed: Added ItemStateTable it_803F7548 with callbacks it_802BE408 and it_802BE458.

Implemented it_802BE214 to fill a SpawnItem, spawn via Item_80268B18, initialize the spawned object through it_802BE2E8, register debug/link data with db_80225DD8, and return the spawned Item_GObj or NULL.
Implemented it_802BE2E8 to initialize owner, lifetime, itcmd var, random speed and angle, x/y velocity, model/item setup calls, effect spawn 0x4D3, and animation update.
Implemented it_802BE408 as the per-frame timer/despawn check, including early completion when xDAC_itcmd_var0 is nonzero and clamping xD44_lifeTimer to 0.0F on expiration.
Implemented it_802BE458 to decay x40_vel.x and x40_vel.y using itPeachToadSporeAttributes.x8_speed_decay_rate.
Implemented four cleanup-style callbacks it_802BE488, it_802BE4AC, it_802BE4D0, and it_802BE4F4, each destroying all effects with efLib_DestroyAll and returning true.
Implemented it_802BE518 as a direct wrapper around itColl_BounceOffShield.
Implemented it_802BE538 to call it_80273030 while preserving xD44_lifeTimer and returning false.
Added includes needed for typed implementation: forward headers, common_structs, random, db, eflib, efsync, item inline/item headers, it_2725, and math.h.
Added itPeachToadSporeAttributes to itCommonItems.h with fields x0_min_speed, x4_max_speed_offset, x8_speed_decay_rate, and xc_angle.
Updated itpeachtoadspore.h prototypes for it_802BE214, it_802BE2E8, it_802BE408, and it_802BE458 to more concrete argument and return types.
Updated configure.py so itpeachtoadspore.c is built as Matching instead of NonMatching.
smart_moves: Introduced a small item-specific special-attributes struct before using xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes, avoiding raw offset guesses in the C implementation.
Kept the spawn path explicit by fully initializing SpawnItem fields, including parent gobj fields, facing direction, zero velocity, damage, x44_flag.b0, and x40.
Used HSD_Randf twice to separate random speed selection from random angle selection, matching the observed behavior without over-collapsing expressions.
Preserved xD44_lifeTimer across it_80273030 in it_802BE538, a matching-sensitive detail for collision/ground-related callbacks.
Kept the apparently redundant if/else around efSync_Spawn(0x4D3, ...) even though both branches do the same call, likely because retaining source structure helped matching.
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS and enum_t/float where evidence allowed, improving future callsite type propagation.
decomp_lessons: For small item files, matching often requires solving both behavior functions and header/build metadata in one PR: implement code, type prototypes, add attribute structs, and flip configure.py only after all functions match.
Article specialAttributes should be modeled with named structs once field meaning is inferred; this makes velocity/timer formulas clearer and reusable.

Do not remove redundant-looking control flow if it is present in the matched shape; the identical efSync_Spawn branches in it_802BE2E8 are a useful reminder that source simplification can break matching.

Callbacks that look like simple wrappers can still encode important engine conventions: destroy effects on multiple termination paths, bounce off shield via itColl_BounceOffShield, and preserve timers around shared collision helpers.
Bot match reports are useful evidence for function-level validation when no human review comments are present.
naming_conventions: Item special-attributes structs use item-specific CamelCase with the suffix Attributes, as in itPeachToadSporeAttributes.
Attribute field names retain offset prefixes and descriptive names: x0_min_speed, x4_max_speed_offset, x8_speed_decay_rate, xc_angle.
Unidentified functions retain address-style names such as it_802BE214, it_802BE2E8, and it_802BE408 while prototypes become typed when possible.
Item enum names follow the It_Kind_* convention, shown by It_Kind_Peach_ToadSpore.
Item state tables use address-style global names, shown by it_803F7548.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use f32 constants with explicit suffixes such as 0.0F, 0.5F, and 60.0F.
Use math.h M_PI cast to f32 for angle computation: double_angle = ((f32) M_PI) - attr->xc_angle.
Compute velocity with facing_dir * (speed * sinf(angle)) for x and speed * cosf(angle) for y.
Preserve local variable staging such as item_1/item_2, item_jobj/hsd_jobj, rand, speed, angle_offset, double_angle, and angle when it helps register/order matching.
Initialize chained vector components with spawn.vel.x = spawn.vel.y = spawn.vel.z = 0.0F.
Return NULL explicitly after failed Item_80268B18 spawn.
Keep xD44_lifeTimer decrement and <= 0.0F comparison structure in the anim callback.
Preserve duplicated efSync_Spawn calls across an if/else branch despite identical behavior.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code +0.02% (+868 bytes), linked code +0.02% (+900 bytes), and 10 new 100% function matches for itpeachtoadspore.
follow_up_queries: Look for other Peach item articles using xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes to compare attribute struct naming and field layouts.
Search for item callbacks that preserve xD44_lifeTimer around it_80273030 for similar collision matching patterns.
Search for redundant identical efSync_Spawn if/else branches in matched item files before simplifying decomp source.
Compare SpawnItem initialization in neighboring item files such as itpeachturnip.c or itpeachparasol.c for reusable spawn boilerplate.
